generated_by: Claude (one-time question-bank generation from extracted criteria)
review_status: DRAFT — requires clinical coordinator review before use

_legend.polarity: requirement = study NEEDS this answer (a 'no' disqualifies);  exclusion = a 'yes' disqualifies
_legend.knockout_power: high = defines who the study is for / hard safety gate;  medium / low = rarer
_legend.tier: core = the standard pre-screen set;  optional = valid but lower-yield, platform can enable
_legend.disqualify_if: the condition that should rule the patient OUT for THIS study
_legend.from_criteria: the protocol criterion IDs this question came from (see criteria.json)
_legend.gender_filter: if set, only ask this question to patients of that sex

intro.text: We're enrolling for a paid weight-management and type 2 diabetes study. Would you like to answer a few quick questions to see if you may qualify?

questions.disqualify_if: (used with weight to compute BMI — see derived.bmi)
(used with height to compute BMI — see derived.bmi)

questions.text: Have you been diagnosed with type 2 diabetes?
questions.note: The protocol also requires an HbA1c 6.5–10% lab value — confirmed at the clinic visit, not asked here.

questions.text: Have you ever tried to lose weight with diet or exercise but were not successful?
Have you ever been diagnosed with type 1 diabetes, a diabetic coma, or diabetic ketoacidosis?
In the last 6 months, have you used a GLP-1 medication (such as Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro, or Zepbound) or any other injectable medication for diabetes or weight loss?
questions.allowed_exceptions: Short-term insulin for under 14 days (e.g. surgery, hospitalization, acute illness) is allowed.
questions.note: Highly relevant for a GLP-1 trial and patients know their meds; covers the injectable-therapy and GLP-1 washout criteria.

questions.text: Have you ever had an organ transplant, or are you waiting for one? (A cornea transplant is OK — answer no for that.)
Have you ever been told you have severe stomach-emptying problems, like gastroparesis or a blocked stomach?
Do you or any close blood relative (parent, sibling, or child) have a history of medullary thyroid cancer or Multiple Endocrine Neoplasia type 2 (MEN2)?
Have you been diagnosed with severe heart failure (NYHA class IV)?
In the last 3 months, have you had a heart attack, a stroke or mini-stroke (TIA), unstable angina, or been hospitalized for heart failure?
Are you currently pregnant or breastfeeding, or planning to become pregnant during the study?
Have you had, or are you planning to have, weight-loss (bariatric) surgery?
questions.allowed_exceptions: Liposuction or abdominoplasty more than 1 year ago is allowed.

questions.text: In the past 5 years, have you had cancer that was active or being treated? (Minor skin cancers and a few in-situ cancers don't count.)
questions.allowed_exceptions: Basal/squamous cell skin cancer, in-situ cervical carcinoma, and in-situ prostate cancer are allowed.

questions.text: Have you ever had pancreatitis (inflammation of the pancreas) or significant gallbladder disease?
questions.allowed_exceptions: Gallstone pancreatitis/gallbladder disease is allowed if the gallbladder was removed at least 3 months ago.

questions.text: Are you currently taking any medication, supplement, or product to help you lose or gain weight?
In the last 3 months, have you taken part in another clinical trial or taken an investigational/experimental drug?
This study involves a weekly self-injection, finger-stick blood sugar checks, and visits over about 18 months. Are you willing and able to take part in all of that?

dropped_criteria_note: All other criteria were intentionally NOT turned into questions because a patient cannot reliably self-report them — they are lab values (HbA1c, GFR, ALT/AST, calcitonin, triglycerides, fasting BG), administered scores (PHQ-9, C-SSRS), exam/imaging findings (retinopathy, dilated fundoscopy), a measured vital (blood pressure), or require investigator judgment. These are checked at the in-clinic screening visit, not in the pre-screen.
